blocks.content: She met me there. No hesitation, no looking away first. Just — there. Her eyes closed. Mine did too. It lasted less than five seconds and felt like something I would remember for a long time.
When I pulled back she was still close. Neither of us said anything. There was nothing to say that would have made it better.
She smiled. A small one. Like she understood exactly what had almost happened and she was going to let it be enough — for now.
She looked at me for just a moment — like what I'd said had weight and she was deciding how to hold it.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: I really like you too, Larry.
I watched her go. Down the steps, into the street, toward her bus stop. She didn't look back this time but I didn't need her to.
The steps were empty. The light was going. And something in me had settled — not finished, not answered, just settled. Like a window closing on a warm room.
